claim: Cross-check confirms: YOLO demand evidence dispatch correctly separates external research evidence from target-project facts through multi-layer validation, automatic scope classification, and enforcement blockers.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt explicitly states: Every evidence record must include scope; project facts require project-scoped evidence with path locators; external research must be marked scope=external
evidenceScopeErrors validates each evidence record has declared valid scope value
normalizeAgentResult applies evidenceScopeErrors and blocks with EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED when scope validation fails
DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION documents: scope is project|external|user|unknown; project facts require project-scoped evidence with path
evidenceRecordScope classifies scope by declared value, URL patterns (https:// becomes external), source types, and path presence
projectEvidenceBlockingIssues generates PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED or PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blockers when project facts lack project-scoped path evidence
evidence.why: Enforcement mechanism preventing external evidence for project facts

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness calls projectEvidenceBlockingIssues for each completed evidence result
evidence.why: Integration of enforcement into PRD readiness gate

evidence.summary: Doctrine explicitly requires: evidence records must declare scope; project evidence requires repo-relative path; external evidence cannot prove target-project facts
Test verifies agent evidence without scope declaration is blocked with EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED error
Test confirms claude research profile is supported while maintaining boundary enforcement
evidence.why: External research tools can be used safely

evidence.summary: Evidence schema includes url field for external evidence when scope is external
evidence.why: API schema supports external URL evidence

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope automatically classifies records with https:// URLs as external scope

risks: If scope validation or classification logic has bugs, external evidence could be mistaken for project evidence, compromising PRD readiness

result.notes: Independent cross-check confirms all 12 evidence citations from explorer are accurate. Scope separation is enforced at four layers: (1) protocol contract in agent prompt, (2) runtime validation in normalizeAgentResult, (3) automatic classification in evidenceRecordScope, (4) PRD readiness enforcement in projectEvidenceBlockingIssues. WebFetch/WebSearch results are automatically classified as external and cannot satisfy project-fact requirements without repo-relative path evidence.
